step.title: Step 35
step.actionDisplay: UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> UP -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT -> RIGHT

sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 30 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions (first 10): UP, UP, UP, UP, UP, UP, UP, UP, UP, UP.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 162, level 2.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- Plan: Try moving agent with arrow keys toward orange target
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 141 actions; a strong score needs about 81 or fewer. You are 1.7x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The agent didn't move with UP or RIGHT keys. The agent is still at (57, 6). Looking at the image, the orange target has moved to the right side of the path. This is interesting - the target seems to have moved.
Let me reconsider the game mechanics. The agent is at the bottom left, and the orange target is on the path. Maybe the agent needs to follow the path to reach the target.
Looking at the path structure:
- The path goes from the bottom left, up, right, down, and then to the orange target
- The agent is at the start of the path
- The orange target is at the end of the path
Maybe I need to use different controls to move the agent along the path. Let me try clicking on the path segments or using different actions.
Actually, wait. Looking at the image more carefully, the orange target is now at a different position than before. Before it was at (34, 32), but now it seems to be further to the right. Let me check the current position of the orange target.
Also, I notice the agent is purple with a white center. This might indicate the agent is in a different state. Maybe the agent needs to be "activated" or "selected" before it can move.
Let me try clicking on the agent first, then using arrow keys.
Actually, let me first undo all the recent actions to get back to a clean state, and then try a different approach. I'll use ACTION7 multiple times to undo.
Wait, ACTION7 only undoes one action at a time. I've made 30 actions (UP and RIGHT), so I'd need to call ACTION7 30 times to get back. That's not efficient.
Let me try a different approach. Maybe the agent moves by clicking on adjacent path segments. Let me try clicking on the path near the agent.
Actually, looking at the image, the agent is at the bottom left corner, and there's a path going up and then right. The orange target is on the right side of the path. Maybe I need to click on the path segments to move the agent along the path.
Let me try clicking on the path segment above the agent.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model updated:
- Arrow keys don't move the agent
- Agent stuck at (57, 6)
- Orange target moved along path
- Maybe need to click on path segments to move agent
Plan: Undo recent useless actions, then try clicking on path to move agent

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Undo the 30 useless UP/RIGHT actions
for i in range(30):
    result = action(['ACTION7'])
    if result.get('done'):
        break

seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
p_nodes = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'p']
O_nodes = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'O']
N_nodes = [n for n in seg if n['color'] == 'N']

print(f"After undo: N={len(N_nodes)}")
if p_nodes:
    b = p_nodes[0]['boundary']
    center_r = (min(x[0] for x in b) + max(x[0] for x in b)) // 2
    center_c = (min(x[1] for x in b) + max(x[1] for x in b)) // 2
    print(f"Agent at ({center_r}, {center_c})")
if O_nodes:
    b = O_nodes[0]['boundary']
    center_r = (min(x[0] for x in b) + max(x[0] for x in b)) // 2
    center_c = (min(x[1] for x in b) + max(x[1] for x in b)) // 2
    print(f"O at ({center_r}, {center_c})")

# List all nodes to understand the path
print(f"\nAll nodes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    center_r = (min(x[0] for x in b) + max(x[0] for x in b)) // 2
    center_c = (min(x[1] for x in b) + max(x[1] for x in b)) // 2
    print(f"  {n['color']} at ({center_r}, {center_c}), pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Agent at (57, 6)
O at (34, 32)
